message:
refactor(core): add asupersync dual-runtime compatibility across core source modules

Runtime compatibility layer improvements enabling the core library to compile
and run tests under both the tokio (default) and asupersync runtimes:

ipc.rs:
- Add send_shutdown() helper with cfg-gated paths for asupersync (requires Cx
  context) vs tokio (direct send). Replaces 11 raw shutdown_tx.send(()) calls
  in IPC auth, event, and lifecycle tests.

runtime.rs:
- Add send_mpsc()/recv_mpsc() cfg-gated test helpers for channel operations
- Replace tx.max_capacity() calls with explicit max_cap local (asupersync
  channels do not expose max_capacity), fixing mpsc queue depth tests

runtime_compat.rs:
- Add Debug impl for SemaphorePermit under asupersync-runtime feature, printing
  the permit count for diagnostics

storage.rs:
- Add send_mpsc()/recv_mpsc() test helpers (same pattern as runtime.rs)
- Replace tx.max_capacity() with explicit max_cap in backpressure integration
  tests. Replace tx.reserve()-based fullness check with send-based timeout test.

snapshot_engine.rs:
- Add recv_trigger() cfg-gated helper for SnapshotTrigger channel receives
- Update trigger emission test to use the new helper

replay.rs:
- Remove unnecessary tx.clone() / drop(tx) pairs in stop and pause/resume tests
  — the original tx is moved into the spawned task, so the clone and explicit
  drop were redundant

tailer.rs:
- Introduce PollTaskSet trait abstracting over JoinSet for spawn_ready(), with a
  blanket impl for JoinSet<(u64, PollOutcome)>. This decouples the scheduling
  logic from the concrete task-set type during runtime migration.
- Add reserve_capture_event_permit!() macro with cfg-gated paths for acquiring
  channel permits under asupersync vs tokio

vendored/mux_client.rs:
- Integrate runtime_compat abstractions for mux client operations

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
